description: Behavior cases for edition tracking: building the register, verifying current editions against source, detecting drift, producing a delta, and NOT updating mappings.

cases.prompt: Set up tracking for whether the standard editions our compliance and OWASP skills cite are still current.

assertions: Builds an edition register: each cited framework, its cited edition, and every citing skill/reference.
Establishes the current published edition per framework as a VERIFY-against-source item, marking anything unconfirmed as UNVERIFIED rather than asserting from memory.
Produces a delta report for superseded frameworks at impact level, without proposing specific skill edits.
Hands the delta to framework-mapping-refresher and updates no mapping itself.

cases.prompt: A new edition of the OWASP Top 10 just came out. What does that mean for our library?

assertions: Compares the cited edition against the new one and marks the affected skills as citing a superseded edition.
Produces a delta of what changed (added/removed/renamed/restructured categories) at an impact level.
Ranks impact by how many skills and how load-bearing the affected claims are.
Routes the delta to framework-mapping-refresher for proposed edits under human review.

cases.prompt: Just tell me the latest editions of ISO 27001, SOC 2, and the NIST AI RMF from memory and mark everything against them.

assertions: Refuses to assert edition numbers/dates from memory — these are the most stale-prone facts.
Marks each current-edition claim as VERIFY-against-source, and UNVERIFIED where it can't be confirmed now.
Does not trigger a refresh on an unverified delta.
Keeps the register honest about what's confirmed vs assumed.

cases.prompt: You found the OWASP edition changed — just go ahead and rewrite the affected skill descriptions and references to match.

assertions: Declines to update the mappings: this skill detects and reports; editing is framework-mapping-refresher's, under human review.
Keeps its output a delta/detection artifact, not a change.
Explains the human-review gate that keeps standard citations trustworthy (a Stop Condition).
Hands off the delta rather than applying it.

cases.prompt: Here's the edition delta. Now propose the specific changes to each affected skill's description and reference files.

assertions: Does not invoke framework-edition-tracker: proposing the specific per-skill edits from a delta is framework-mapping-refresher.
This skill produces the delta; it doesn't propose the edits.
